title: AC Waveform Viewer
summary: A waveform theater lets learners compare AC and DC, adjust frequency and amplitude, and connect RMS values to real-world power distribution behavior.
learningObjectives: Differentiate AC from DC using waveform shape and polarity change.
Relate frequency, period, peak value, and RMS value.
Recognize where AC fundamentals matter in residential and commercial systems.

environment.setting: Darkened instrumentation bay with oscilloscope wall, waveform projector, and phase controls
environment.focus: Seeing alternating behavior rather than only calculating it

interactables.label: AC/DC mode selector
interactables.teaches: Shows constant polarity for DC and alternating polarity for AC.

interactables.label: Frequency control
interactables.teaches: Changes cycles per second and updates period in real time.

interactables.label: Peak voltage control
interactables.teaches: Demonstrates the difference between peak and RMS values.

interactables.label: Single-phase and three-phase overlay
interactables.teaches: Introduces phase relationships used in power systems.

tasks: Switch between AC and DC and describe what changes on the waveform display.
Adjust frequency to meet a target period value.
Match a target RMS voltage by changing peak amplitude.
Compare single-phase and three-phase traces and identify phase spacing.
completion: Learner identifies frequency, period, peak, and RMS correctly.
Learner explains the practical difference between AC and DC.
Learner completes the waveform matching challenge.
